name: Franklin D. Roosevelt
role: US President (1933–1945); Commander-in-Chief
quote: Yesterday, December 7th, 1941 — a date which will live in infamy — the United States of America was suddenly and deliberately attacked by naval and air forces of the Empire of Japan.

name: Winston Churchill
role: British Prime Minister (1940–1945); First Lord of the Admiralty (1939–40)
quote: We shall fight on the beaches, we shall fight on the landing grounds, we shall fight in the fields and in the streets, we shall fight in the hills; we shall never surrender.

name: Joseph Stalin
role: Soviet General Secretary; Supreme Commander of Soviet Armed Forces
quote: The Red Army and the Soviet people have together delivered a crushing blow to German fascism, thereby paving the way for the final victory over the enemy.

name: Adolf Hitler
role: Nazi Germany Führer and Chancellor; Supreme Commander of German Armed Forces
quote: Germany will either be a world power or will not be at all. — Mein Kampf, 1925

name: Harry S. Truman
role: US President (April–December 1945); authorized use of atomic bombs
quote: We have used it in order to shorten the agony of war, in order to save the lives of thousands and thousands of young Americans. We shall continue to use it until we completely destroy Japan's power to make war.

name: Dwight D. Eisenhower
role: Supreme Allied Commander Europe (SHAEF); led D-Day and Western Allied forces
quote: You are about to embark upon the Great Crusade, toward which we have striven these many months. The eyes of the world are upon you. — D-Day Order of the Day, June 5, 1944

name: George S. Patton
role: US 3rd Army Commander; led breakout from Normandy and relief of Bastogne
quote: No bastard ever won a war by dying for his country. He won it by making the other poor dumb bastard die for his country.

name: Douglas MacArthur
role: US Commander Southwest Pacific Area; accepted Japan's surrender on USS Missouri
quote: I shall return. — Upon leaving the Philippines, March 1942

name: Georgy Zhukov
role: Soviet Marshal; commanded defense of Moscow, Stalingrad, Kursk, and Berlin
quote: The Nazis had boasted that this would be a lightning war, a war of several weeks. Now, after more than four years, the fascist beast has been slaughtered in his own lair.

name: Charles de Gaulle
role: Leader of Free France; head of French government-in-exile in London
quote: France has lost a battle. But France has not lost the war! — Appeal of 18 June, 1940

name: Benito Mussolini
role: Italian Duce (leader); co-founder of the Axis, overthrown July 1943
quote: It is better to live one day as a lion than a hundred years as a sheep.

name: Hideki Tōjō
role: Japanese Prime Minister (1941–1944); Army General; approved Pearl Harbor attack
quote: A lack of planning is a fatal weakness. — Attributed in post-war accounts

name: Emperor Hirohito
role: Emperor of Japan (Shōwa); announced Japan's surrender August 15, 1945
quote: The war situation has developed not necessarily to Japan's advantage. We must endure the unendurable and suffer what is insufferable. — Surrender Broadcast, August 15, 1945

name: Erwin Rommel
role: German Field Marshal; 'Desert Fox,' commanded Afrika Korps and D-Day defenses
quote: The first 24 hours of the invasion will be decisive. The fate of Germany depends on the outcome... For the Allies as well as Germany, it will be the longest day.

name: Bernard Montgomery
role: British Field Marshal; commanded 8th Army at El Alamein and Allied ground forces at D-Day
quote: The German is a good soldier. It is an equal fight. But we will win because our morale is better and we fight for a better cause.

name: Heinrich Himmler
role: SS Reichsführer; chief architect of the Holocaust and concentration camp system
quote: We have the moral right, we had the duty to our people to do it, to kill this people who wanted to kill us. — Posen Speech, October 4, 1943

name: Isoroku Yamamoto
role: Japanese Admiral; planned Pearl Harbor attack; killed in 1943 US ambush
quote: I fear all we have done is to awaken a sleeping giant and fill him with a terrible resolve. — Attributed (authenticity disputed)

name: Albert Speer
role: Nazi Minister of Armaments (1942–45); organized German war production; sentenced at Nuremberg
quote: Hitler was a demonic figure... The first target of his totalitarian ambitions were the German people. — Inside the Third Reich, 1970

name: Chester Nimitz
role: US Admiral; Commander-in-Chief Pacific Fleet; oversaw Pacific island-hopping campaign
quote: Among the men who fought on Iwo Jima, uncommon valor was a common virtue.

name: William Slim
role: British General; commanded 14th Army ('Forgotten Army') reconquest of Burma
quote: When you cannot make up your mind which of two evenly balanced courses of action you should take — choose the bolder.

name: Konstantin Rokossovsky
role: Soviet Marshal; commanded Operation Bagration — the largest Allied offensive of WWII
quote: The fascist beast has been broken. Our great people have achieved a victory that will go down in the annals of history.

name: Reinhard Heydrich
role: SS-Obergruppenführer; chaired Wannsee Conference; organized the Final Solution
quote: In the course of the practical execution of the Final Solution, Europe will be combed through from west to east. — Wannsee Protocol, January 20, 1942

name: Chiang Kai-shek
role: Chinese Nationalist Leader (Generalissimo); led China's war against Japan 1937–1945
quote: We did not go to war to conquer or enslave the Japanese people, but to resist the aggressors, and to free ourselves from oppression.

name: Vyacheslav Molotov
role: Soviet Foreign Minister; signed Molotov–Ribbentrop Pact; represented USSR at Allied conferences
quote: The Soviet government considers it necessary to tell the peoples of the Soviet Union: The war with Germany has not ended because Germany signed capitulation — it has ended because the working people of Germany triumphed.

name: Neville Chamberlain
role: British Prime Minister (1937–1940); signed Munich Agreement; replaced by Churchill
quote: My good friends, for the second time in our history, a British Prime Minister has returned from Germany bringing peace with honour. I believe it is peace for our time. — September 30, 1938
